sql_snippet_patterns.description: Templates for high-value Genie SQL snippets. Replace placeholders with actual table/column names from the metadata.

time_filters.note: Filter to the current calendar month

analytical_measures.note: Each row's share of the overall total

analytical_measures.sql: SUM({value_col}) OVER (ORDER BY {date_col} ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)
AVG({value_col}) OVER (ORDER BY {date_col} ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)

analytical_measures.note: Rank entities by a value descending

entity_measures.sql: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN {repeat_condition} THEN {entity_id_col} END) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT {entity_id_col}), 0)
entity_measures.note: Fraction of entities that meet a repeat/return condition

entity_measures.sql: SUM(CASE WHEN rn <= 10 THEN {value_col} ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM({value_col}), 0)
entity_measures.note: Concentration metric -- what fraction comes from top N

instruction_templates.description: Templates for writing high-quality Genie text instructions. Fill in bracketed placeholders with actual schema details.
instruction_templates.data_overview: This space contains data about {domain_description}. The main fact table is {fact_table} which tracks {what_it_tracks}. Dimension tables include {dim_tables_list} which provide {what_they_provide}.
relationship_rules: The {fact_table} table has one row per {grain}. Aggregations should group by the appropriate dimension.
Date columns like {date_col} store {timezone_info}. Use DATE_TRUNC for period grouping.
business_rules: Revenue should be calculated from {revenue_col} and only include rows where {revenue_filter}.
When users ask about '{ambiguous_term}', they mean {precise_definition}.
disambiguation: Column '{col_name}' in {table_a} refers to {meaning_a}, while '{col_name}' in {table_b} refers to {meaning_b}.
If the user asks for '{common_question}', query {correct_table} not {wrong_table}.

example_sql_patterns.description: Templates for example_sql pairs that exercise joins, aggregations, date ranges, and analytical queries. Much more valuable than trivial SELECT * queries.

aggregation_with_join.question: What is {measure} by {dimension_from_dim_table}?
aggregation_with_join.sql: SELECT {dim_table}.{dim_col}, {agg_func}({fact_table}.{measure_col}) AS {alias} FROM {fact_table} JOIN {dim_table} ON {fact_table}.{fk} = {dim_table}.{pk} GROUP BY {dim_table}.{dim_col} ORDER BY {alias} DESC

time_series.question: What is the {measure} trend by month?
time_series.sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', {date_col}) AS month, {agg_func}({measure_col}) AS {alias} FROM {table} WHERE {date_col} >= TIMESTAMPADD(MONTH, -12, current_date()) GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

top_n.question: Who are the top 10 {entities} by {measure}?
top_n.sql: SELECT {entity_col}, {agg_func}({measure_col}) AS {alias} FROM {table} GROUP BY {entity_col} ORDER BY {alias} DESC LIMIT 10

comparison.question: How does {measure} compare between {category_a} and {category_b}?
comparison.sql: SELECT {category_col}, {agg_func}({measure_col}) AS {alias} FROM {table} WHERE {category_col} IN ('{category_a}', '{category_b}') GROUP BY {category_col}

filtered_with_having.question: Which {entities} have more than {threshold} {measure}?
filtered_with_having.sql: SELECT {entity_col}, {agg_func}({measure_col}) AS {alias} FROM {table} GROUP BY {entity_col} HAVING {alias} > {threshold} ORDER BY {alias} DESC

multi_table_analytical.question: What is the average {measure} per {entity} by {dim_from_another_table}?
multi_table_analytical.sql: SELECT {dim_table}.{dim_col}, {agg_func}({fact_table}.{measure_col}) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT {fact_table}.{entity_id}), 0) AS avg_{alias}_per_{entity} FROM {fact_table} JOIN {dim_table} ON {fact_table}.{fk} = {dim_table}.{pk} GROUP BY {dim_table}.{dim_col} ORDER BY avg_{alias}_per_{entity} DESC

sample_question_patterns.description: Question templates that test different SQL capabilities. Generate domain-specific variants by filling in the placeholders.

aggregation: What is the average {measure} across all {entities}?
time_series: How has {measure} changed over the last 12 months?
What is the {measure} trend by quarter this year?
comparison: What is the breakdown of {measure} by {categorical_dimension}?
filtering: Show me {entities} where {column} is greater than {threshold}

date_function_rules.description: Consolidated Databricks/Spark SQL date function reference. These rules apply to both metric view YAML expressions and Genie SQL snippets.

DATE_TRUNC.note: Unit MUST be single-quoted. Returns a timestamp truncated to the specified precision.

EXTRACT.note: Unit is a bare keyword with FROM. Do NOT use DATE_PART.

DATEDIFF.note: Returns integer days only. Two arguments only. For other units, use TIMESTAMPDIFF.

TIMESTAMPDIFF.note: Unit is a bare keyword, singular, NO quotes.

TIMESTAMPADD.note: Unit is a bare keyword, singular, NO quotes.

date_sub.note: Subtracts integer days from a date. Simpler than TIMESTAMPADD for day arithmetic.

current_date.note: Returns today's date. Always include parentheses.
